companies.name: Bloomberg
companies.edge: PR on BDE before applying. 5 different teams hire C++.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.edge: Many sub-orgs. CUDA libs + AV stack both hire C++ heavily.

companies.name: Citadel / Citadel Securities
companies.h1bNote: Heavy H1B sponsor at senior IC
companies.edge: Note: Citadel HF and Citadel Securities have different career portals.

companies.name: Hudson River Trading
companies.h1bNote: Heavy sponsor, strongest TC band in HFT
companies.edge: Their 'Core' team hires year-round.

companies.name: Jump Trading
companies.edge: FPGA-adjacent infra; mention if you've touched HW/SW boundary.

companies.name: Optiver
companies.edge: Chicago + Austin + NYC offices.

companies.name: ClickHouse
companies.edge: Hyper-growth ($15B). OSS PR + cover letter = instant signal.

companies.name: ScyllaDB
companies.edge: Seastar framework — niche C++ skillset; if you build there you're hireable.

companies.name: Latitude AI (Ford)
companies.h1bNote: Already applied — watch for new postings

companies.name: Anduril
companies.h1bNote: Many roles US-persons; some (sim, infra) sponsor

companies.name: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE / NYSE)
companies.h1bNote: Sponsors. Hidden gem — interview-signal source: LeetCode + TeamBlind
companies.edge: NYSE matching engines. Less applicant flood vs HFT firms — apply direct via careers.ice.com.

companies.name: Susquehanna International Group (SIG)
companies.h1bNote: Heavy C++ shop, Bala Cynwyd HQ. 8+ active C++ reqs.
companies.edge: Often overshadowed by Citadel/Jane Street. Strong C++ culture; multiple trading-tech teams hiring continuously.

companies.name: D. E. Shaw Group
companies.edge: Options group hiring 3+ yrs C++. Less LeetCode-grindy than HFT firms.

companies.name: CrowdStrike
companies.h1bNote: Top H1B sponsor ($193K median LCA). ~105 senior SWE reqs.
companies.edge: Kernel-adjacent C/C++ on Windows/Linux/macOS. Your Samsung mesh-router C/C++ networking is directly analogous.

companies.name: Autodesk
companies.h1bNote: Sponsors. AutoCAD/Maya/Revit are massive C++ codebases.
companies.edge: $222K median TC. Often overlooked vs FAANG. Hiring across 88+ SWE roles.

companies.name: Adobe
companies.h1bNote: Top H1B sponsor. Photoshop/Lightroom/Acrobat are C++.
companies.edge: Layoffs in 2026 created market churn. Many open reqs across Creative Cloud.

companies.name: Honeycomb.io
companies.h1bNote: Series D, remote-friendly. Storage team adding C++.

companies.name: Nasdaq
companies.h1bNote: Sponsors. Heavy C++ in matching engines.

companies.name: NBCUniversal
companies.h1bNote: Streaming + ad tech in C++. Confirmed C++ interview signal (TeamBlind).

companies.name: Belvedere Trading
companies.h1bNote: Smaller Chicago MM. Confirmed Senior C++ SWE at $175-250K.
companies.edge: Less applicant-flooded than Citadel/Optiver. TeamBlind says interview bar is lower.

companies.name: Wolverine Trading
companies.h1bNote: Chicago options HFT. Lower comp but lower bar.

companies.name: PEAK6 Investments
companies.h1bNote: Chicago options. Parent of PEAK6 Capital + Apex (clearing).

companies.name: Virtu Financial
companies.edge: Less LeetCode-grindy than top HFTs.

companies.name: Riot Games
companies.edge: League of Legends, Valorant — huge C++ codebases. Online services team hires year-round.

companies.name: Activision Blizzard (Microsoft)
companies.h1bNote: Owned by Microsoft → top H1B sponsor. 97 US reqs.

companies.name: Synopsys
companies.h1bNote: Top H1B sponsor. Now owns Ansys.
companies.edge: EDA C++ — niche skill but very steady demand. Less competition than FAANG.

companies.name: Arista Networks
companies.h1bNote: Sponsors US roles. CloudEOS team takes 3+ yrs C++.

companies.name: Cisco
companies.h1bNote: Top H1B sponsor. 56 SWE roles open.
companies.edge: Skew is now to AI networking fabric + custom silicon — strong fit for systems C++.

companies.name: Discord
companies.h1bNote: Sponsors. Heavy C++/Rust in A/V stack.

companies.name: Axon
companies.edge: Computer vision + edge devices. Less applicant flood than FAANG CV roles.

companies.name: Network Optix
companies.edge: C++23 + latest Clang/MSVC. Niche stack = small applicant pool.

companies.name: Figure AI
companies.edge: Humanoid robotics gold rush — C++ on robot, real-time embedded.

companies.name: Torc Robotics
companies.h1bNote: Daimler-backed. Top H1B sponsor in AV.
companies.edge: Multiple senior C++ reqs open at any time. Ann Arbor low-applicant-flood.

edgeTactics.title: DM the hiring manager, not the recruiter
edgeTactics.detail: On LinkedIn, find the team's tech lead or EM (search company + team name, e.g., 'Bloomberg BQL Kernel'). Send a 3-line note: 1) you just applied, 2) 1 concrete line tying your Wabtec real-time work to their stack, 3) ask if they'd want a 15-min call. 5–10x higher reply rate than cold-apply.

edgeTactics.title: Drop a public C++ artifact this week
edgeTactics.detail: Push ONE focused repo to github.com/[your-github] before next Mon: a lock-free SPSC queue, a tiny order-book matching engine, or a sensor-fusion mini-demo. Link it in the resume header and every cover letter. Hiring loops will skim it in 30 seconds — beats years of bullet points.

edgeTactics.title: File a 1-line PR on the company's OSS repo
edgeTactics.detail: Bloomberg → github.com/bloomberg/bde · ClickHouse → github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse · MongoDB → github.com/mongodb/mongo · ScyllaDB → github.com/scylladb/scylladb. Even a typo fix gets you a real commit history with the org. Mention it in the cover letter.

edgeTactics.title: Referral mining via Binghamton + Samsung + Wabtec
edgeTactics.detail: LinkedIn search: 'Binghamton University' + the target company, also 'Samsung' alumni and 'Wabtec' alumni. Most companies pay $2–5K per referral so people will respond. DM template: 'Hey [name], saw you went to Binghamton too. I'm applying to [role] — would you be open to forwarding my resume internally?'

edgeTactics.title: Specialised HFT/finance recruiters
edgeTactics.detail: Skip generic recruiters. DM these on LinkedIn — they have unposted HFT C++ roles: Selby Jennings, Durlston Partners (already in your list), Glocomms, Mondo, Oxford Knight, Anson McCade, Algo Capital Group. One DM each. Position yourself as 'real-time C++ on locomotives — interested in low-latency finance.'

edgeTactics.title: ATS keyword tune per industry
edgeTactics.detail: Your resume is heavy on 'optimization engine' and 'locomotives' — great for AV/robotics, generic for finance. For HFT roles, slip in: kernel bypass, DPDK, Solarflare/EFvi, FIX protocol, ITCH/OUCH, FPGA-adjacent, RDMA, busy-wait, cache-line, NUMA. For AV: ROS2, DDS, TensorRT, ONNX, sensor fusion (you already have), Kalman filter. ATS gates ~40% of these apps.

edgeTactics.title: 1-page tech brief beats cover letter
edgeTactics.detail: For each top-3 target (e.g., Bloomberg BDE, Stevens Capital, HRT), write a 1-page PDF: 'Notes on [their system] from a senior C++ IC.' 4 sections — what I think you're solving, where I see latency wins, what I'd build first month, open questions. Attach to the application or send via DM. Almost no one does this.

edgeTactics.title: Get inbound flowing in parallel
edgeTactics.detail: Sign up on TripleByte, Hired.com, A.Team, and Otta — set them to 'Senior C++, H1B OK, US remote.' Posting profiles takes 60 minutes total and feeds you recruiter pings while you're sleeping. Also: turn LinkedIn 'Open to Work' on with the green ring (recruiter-only view, not public).

edgeTactics.title: H1B leverage line in DMs
edgeTactics.detail: Say 'I'm on H1B with [N] years remaining' once explicitly — companies that can't sponsor will self-eliminate, saving you the pipeline cycle. Companies that CAN will treat it as a non-issue. Don't hide it; it becomes a problem only at offer stage.

edgeTactics.title: Apply within 24h of post going live
edgeTactics.detail: LinkedIn data: apps submitted in the first 24 hours of a posting have ~4x interview rate vs. apps in week 2+. Use LinkedIn 'Date posted: Past 24 hours' filter. Set up Email/Slack alerts on LinkedIn Job Alerts + key Greenhouse/Ashby boards (Stevens Capital, Applied Intuition, Latitude AI, Bloomberg).
